codeReview.summary: The scheduler-work-registry implementation is well-designed and comprehensive. It adds a minimal but complete work registry for tracking spawned tasks with parent-child relationships, state tracking, and result storage. The implementation follows existing codebase patterns, uses proper error handling, and includes thorough test coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the established ErrNotFound pattern from the store package. All CRUD operations properly return ErrNotFound for missing records, consistent with existing store methods.

Worker created comprehensive library documentation in .factory/library/work-registry.md that will be valuable for downstream features (scheduler-spawn-api, scheduler-status-api, runtime-parent-child-channels, etext-research-button).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/work-registry.md - Documents schema, types, store methods, patterns, and downstream feature dependencies

sharedStateObservations.observation: Proper reuse of existing TaskState type for WorkItem.State ensures consistency with the existing task lifecycle states across the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/types/task.go:294 (WorkItem.State uses TaskState), internal/types/task_test.go:301-307 (TestWorkItemUsesTaskState validates all TaskState values work)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed TDD pattern as documented in go-worker SKILL.md - wrote 17 failing tests first, then implemented to make them pass (red-green-refactor).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff transcript shows: tests created first, compilation errors expected, then implementation added to make tests pass

summary: The scheduler-work-registry feature is a solid foundation for the choir-in-choir milestone. It implements: (1) WorkItem type with all required fields including ParentID for parent-child relationships, (2) work_items table with proper indexes on owner_id, parent_id, and state, (3) 6 CRUD store methods with proper error handling, (4) 20 comprehensive tests covering state transitions (pending→running→completed/failed), parent-child isolation, owner scoping, persistence, and concurrent worker patterns. All validators pass (go test, go vet). The worker followed all documented patterns and created helpful library documentation for downstream features.
